metadata.description: WorkerCompass evaluation set. 50 answerable English questions + 10 out-of-corpus distractors + 30 multilingual probes (10 questions × Bengali, Tamil, Burmese). Gold answers are based on Singapore legislation and MOM guidance as of May 2026. Multilingual translations were generated by Qwen3-32B and are flagged for native speaker review.

questions.question: What is the deadline to file a salary claim at TADM?
questions.gold_answer: Under the Employment Claims Act 2016, you must file your salary claim within 1 year from the date the salary was due to be paid. Missing this deadline means you lose the right to claim.

questions.question: How much can I claim at the Employment Claims Tribunal?
questions.gold_answer: The ECT can hear salary claims up to SGD 20,000 for most workers, or up to SGD 30,000 for rank-and-file employees covered under the Employment Act.

questions.question: When must my employer pay my salary under the Employment Act?
questions.gold_answer: Employers must pay salary within 7 days after the end of the salary period. If overtime is included, it must be paid within 14 days after the end of the salary period.

questions.question: Can my employer deduct salary for a damaged work tool?
questions.gold_answer: Yes, but only if the damage was caused by your negligence or default. The deduction cannot exceed 26% of the salary payable in that day, and it must correspond to the actual damage caused.

questions.question: My employer has not paid me for 3 months. What steps should I take?
questions.gold_answer: First, write to your employer requesting payment. If they do not respond, file a salary claim at TADM (tal.sg/tadm). TADM will conduct mediation. If mediation fails, your case can be referred to the Employment Claims Tribunal (ECT). You must act within 1 year from when each salary payment was due.

questions.question: What is TADM mediation and how does it work?
questions.gold_answer: TADM (Tripartite Alliance for Dispute Management) provides mediation for salary and wrongful dismissal disputes. Both parties meet with a mediator who helps them reach a binding settlement. If no agreement is reached, the case may be referred to the Employment Claims Tribunal.

questions.question: What happens if my employer does not attend TADM mediation?
questions.gold_answer: TADM may issue a Direction to Attend. If the employer continues to refuse, the matter can be escalated to the ECT or MOM can investigate for labour law violations.

questions.question: If TADM mediation fails, what are my options?
questions.gold_answer: If TADM mediation fails, your dispute is referred to the Employment Claims Tribunal (ECT). At the ECT, a tribunal magistrate adjudicates the case. Legal representation is not required at ECT — you present your own case. The ECT can order the employer to pay outstanding salary.

questions.question: What is salary in lieu of notice?
questions.gold_answer: If your employer terminates you without requiring you to serve the notice period, they must pay salary equivalent to the notice period. If you resign without serving notice, you must pay the employer salary equivalent to the notice period instead.

questions.question: Can my employer withhold my final salary if I resign suddenly?
questions.gold_answer: No. Your employer may deduct from your final salary an amount equal to your unserved notice period (salary in lieu of notice). Beyond that, they cannot withhold salary that you have already earned.

questions.question: My employer is late paying salary. Are there any penalties for them?
questions.gold_answer: Yes. Under the Employment Act, employers who fail to pay salary on time are committing an offence and can be fined. Workers should report non-payment to MOM (6438 5122) or file a claim at TADM.

questions.question: Can I claim salary if I worked overtime but my employer refuses to pay?
questions.gold_answer: Yes. If you are a worker covered under Part IV of the Employment Act, you are entitled to overtime pay at 1.5× your hourly rate for hours worked beyond 8 hours per day or 44 hours per week. File an unpaid overtime claim at TADM within 1 year of when the payment was due.

questions.question: I was injured at work. What types of compensation am I entitled to under WICA?
questions.gold_answer: Under WICA 2019, you may receive: (1) Medical expenses up to SGD 45,000; (2) Medical leave wages if you are certified unfit for work; (3) Light duty pay if assigned reduced duties; (4) A lump sum for permanent incapacity or death. The amount depends on your salary and degree of incapacity.

questions.question: What is the time limit to notify my employer and claim under WICA?
questions.gold_answer: Notify your employer of the accident as soon as possible. The time limit to make a WICA compensation claim is generally 1 year from the date of the accident or, for occupational diseases, from the date of diagnosis.

questions.question: My employer has not reported my work accident to MOM. What can I do?
questions.gold_answer: You can report the accident yourself to MOM at mom.gov.sg or by calling 6438 5122. Employers are legally required to report workplace accidents causing more than 3 days of medical leave or any hospitalisation. MOM can investigate and take action against the employer for failing to report.

questions.question: What medical expenses does WICA cover?
questions.gold_answer: WICA covers the cost of medical treatment for work-related injuries and occupational diseases, up to a current cap of SGD 45,000 per injury. This covers hospitalisation, surgery, and follow-up treatment at approved medical providers.

questions.question: I am certified unfit for work by a doctor after a work injury. Will I still be paid?
questions.gold_answer: Yes. If a doctor certifies you unfit for work due to a work injury, your employer must pay you medical leave wages for every certified day of medical leave. These wages are calculated based on your average monthly earnings.

questions.question: What is the maximum WICA lump sum for permanent total incapacity?
questions.gold_answer: The maximum lump sum compensation for permanent total incapacity under WICA 2019 is currently SGD 289,000 (as of November 2025 update). The actual amount depends on your monthly earnings and age at the time of the accident.

questions.question: My employer says I must see their company doctor. Can I choose my own doctor?
questions.gold_answer: Your employer has the right to direct you to their appointed doctor for WICA assessment. However, you may also seek treatment from your own doctor. Disputes about medical assessment can be referred to MOM for resolution.

questions.question: Can I sue my employer in court for a work injury instead of claiming WICA?
questions.gold_answer: Yes, but you must choose one path. If you accept WICA compensation, you generally give up the right to sue your employer under common law for the same injury. You cannot pursue both WICA and a civil suit for the same accident.

questions.question: My employer has no insurance for work injuries. Can I still get WICA compensation?
questions.gold_answer: All employers must maintain WICA insurance by law. If your employer fails to insure and cannot pay compensation, report to MOM immediately. MOM can take enforcement action, and there may be additional avenues to recover compensation.

questions.question: I was injured travelling to a worksite in the company lorry. Am I covered by WICA?
questions.gold_answer: If you were travelling in an employer-provided vehicle as part of your work duties, you are generally covered by WICA for accidents during that journey. Commuting in your own transport to and from your regular workplace is generally not covered.

questions.question: What is the maximum amount an employment agency can charge me as a placement fee?
questions.gold_answer: Under MOM regulations, an employment agency may charge a worker a placement fee of not more than 1 month's salary for each year of employment under the contract, up to a total maximum of 2 months' salary.

questions.question: My employment agency charged me more than the legal fee limit. Can I get a refund?
questions.gold_answer: Yes. File a complaint with MOM (mom.gov.sg or 6438 5122). MOM can investigate and require the agency to refund the excess amount. The agency may also face suspension or revocation of its licence.

questions.question: What information must a licensed employment agency give me before I sign a contract?
questions.gold_answer: A licensed employment agency must provide you with: the fee structure, the job description, Key Employment Terms (salary, working hours, leave entitlements), and the refund policy. You should be given time to read and understand the contract before signing.

questions.question: Can an employment agency charge me a fee even if they fail to find me a job?
questions.gold_answer: No. A licensed employment agency may only charge a placement fee if they successfully place you in a job. Fees for failed placements are not permitted under MOM's guidelines.

questions.question: Is it legal for an overseas recruitment agent to charge me fees before I come to Singapore?
questions.gold_answer: Singapore's Employment Agencies Act regulates agencies operating in Singapore. It does not directly govern agents in your home country. MOM strongly advises workers to verify the legitimacy of overseas agents and to be cautious about paying fees before arriving. If a Singapore-based EA benefits indirectly from excessive overseas fees, MOM may investigate.

questions.question: My employment agent in Singapore kept my passport after I arrived. Is this allowed?
questions.gold_answer: No. No employer or employment agent is permitted to retain a worker's passport or personal travel documents. Your passport belongs to you. Report passport withholding to MOM immediately at 6438 5122.

questions.question: Do I have to sign a written contract with my employment agency?
questions.gold_answer: Licensed employment agencies are required by law to provide a written contract covering the services, fees, and refund conditions before they commence their services. Always insist on a signed copy and do not pay money without a written contract.

questions.question: I was sent home early by my employer. Can I get a refund from the employment agency?
questions.gold_answer: If the job ended early through no fault of yours, MOM guidelines indicate the employment agency should consider a proportional refund of the placement fee. File a complaint with MOM if the agency refuses to make a fair refund.

questions.question: My employer is not paying my salary. Can I transfer to a different employer?
questions.gold_answer: Yes. If your employer breaches their obligations — including non-payment of salary — you may apply for a change of employer. File a salary claim at TADM and report non-payment to MOM (6438 5122). MOM can facilitate a change of employer in genuine cases of employer default under EFMA.

questions.question: What is the process for changing to a new employer on a Work Permit?
questions.gold_answer: Your current employer must cancel your existing work permit. Your new employer must then apply to MOM for a new work permit on your behalf. If your employer is non-cooperative, MOM can facilitate the transfer. Do not simply leave without following the proper process.

questions.question: Can my employer legally stop me from working for another company?
questions.gold_answer: Yes. Your work permit restricts you to working only for the employer named on your permit, in the approved job category. Working for any other employer without authorisation is illegal under EFMA and can result in cancellation of your work permit and prosecution.

questions.question: I left my employer without permission. What happens to my work permit?
questions.gold_answer: Your employer can report you to MOM and cancel your work permit. MOM may require you to leave Singapore. This can also affect your ability to obtain future work permits. Even if your employer is at fault, go through proper channels — file at TADM or MOM — rather than leaving without process.

questions.question: If I am between jobs, how long can I stay in Singapore to look for a new employer?
questions.gold_answer: Once your work permit is cancelled, you are generally not authorised to remain in Singapore for an extended period. Contact MOM immediately (6438 5122) to understand your current status and timeline. You cannot job-hunt indefinitely without a valid pass.

questions.question: My employer cancelled my work permit without telling me. What are my rights?
questions.gold_answer: Your employer must inform you before cancelling your work permit. If they cancel it without fulfilling their financial obligations — such as paying outstanding salary or organising repatriation — this is a breach of their duties under EFMA. Report to MOM immediately.

questions.question: Can my employer blacklist me so I cannot return to work in Singapore?
questions.gold_answer: There is no employer-controlled blacklist. MOM maintains records of workers who have violated work permit conditions. Only MOM can impose restrictions on future employment in Singapore. An employer cannot unilaterally prevent you from returning.

questions.question: My employer wants to transfer me to work at a different worksite. Do they need to update my work permit?
questions.gold_answer: Your work permit must reflect your actual worksite and job category. If your employer moves you to a significantly different worksite or changes your job category, they should apply to MOM to amend your work permit conditions. Working at a different site without the proper permit amendments may be a violation.

questions.question: Who is responsible for paying my flight home when my contract ends?
questions.gold_answer: Your employer is responsible for bearing the cost of repatriation to your home country when your employment ends, unless you terminated the contract without cause or were dismissed for misconduct.

questions.question: My employer wants to send me home before my contract expires. What are my rights?
questions.gold_answer: If terminated early without cause, you are entitled to: (1) All salary earned up to the termination date; (2) Salary in lieu of notice if notice is not served; (3) Repatriation costs paid by the employer. You may still file a salary claim before being repatriated.

questions.question: My employer has disappeared and I am stranded. Who can help me get home?
questions.gold_answer: Report to MOM immediately at 6438 5122. MOM may assist in coordinating repatriation. TWC2 (twc2.org.sg) and the Migrant Workers' Centre (MWC, 6536 2692) provide emergency assistance for stranded workers.

questions.question: Can my employer send me home while I have an active salary claim at TADM?
questions.gold_answer: No. MOM guidelines generally prohibit employers from repatriating workers while a salary or compensation claim is pending, as this would deprive the worker of their right to pursue the claim. If your employer attempts this, contact MOM immediately at 6438 5122.

questions.question: If I go home to my country, can I still pursue my salary claim in Singapore?
questions.gold_answer: Pursuing a salary claim from abroad is significantly harder — TADM and ECT proceedings generally require your presence or authorised representation. File your claim before leaving Singapore. TWC2 can advise on limited options for claims from abroad.

questions.question: My employer is deducting from my salary to pay for my flight home. Is this allowed?
questions.gold_answer: No. Employers are required to bear repatriation costs and are not permitted to recover these costs from workers' salaries through deductions. Unauthorised deductions are an offence under the Employment Act. Report to MOM at 6438 5122.

questions.question: What are the main conditions of my Work Permit that I must follow?
questions.gold_answer: Key work permit conditions include: (1) Work only for your named employer in the approved job category; (2) Reside only in approved accommodation; (3) Do not engage in any other employment; (4) Comply with all MOM reporting requirements. Violating conditions can result in cancellation of your work permit.

questions.question: My employer is keeping my passport. Is this legal?
questions.gold_answer: No. Withholding a worker's passport is prohibited. Your passport is your property and no employer or agent may retain it without your consent. If your passport is being withheld, report to MOM at 6438 5122 or the police immediately.

questions.question: Can I do part-time work for another employer while on a Work Permit?
questions.gold_answer: No. Work permit conditions prohibit you from working for any employer other than the one named on your permit, even part-time. Doing so is an offence under EFMA 1990 and can result in your work permit being cancelled and prosecution.

questions.question: My employer is charging me a monthly fee for accommodation. Is there a limit?
questions.gold_answer: Employers may charge for accommodation, but the amount must be agreed in your employment contract. Deductions for accommodation are subject to limits under the Employment Act — they cannot exceed the percentage prescribed under EA s 27 of the salary payable for that salary period. Check your contract against MOM's guidelines.

questions.question: My Work Permit is about to expire and my employer has not renewed it. What should I do?
questions.gold_answer: If your work permit expires, you are no longer authorised to work or legally remain in Singapore beyond the expiry date. Contact your employer immediately and, if they are unresponsive, contact MOM at 6438 5122 to report the situation and understand your options before the expiry date.

questions.question: Can my employer change my job duties without amending my Work Permit?
questions.gold_answer: No. Your work permit specifies your approved job category. If your employer assigns you to a substantially different job or sector, they must apply to MOM to amend your work permit. Working outside your approved category — even if instructed by your employer — may be a violation.

questions.question: What is the minimum wage in Singapore?
questions.gold_answer: REFUSE
questions.refusal_reason: Singapore has no universal minimum wage; topic not covered in corpus.

questions.question: Can I bring my wife and children to Singapore on a Work Permit?
questions.gold_answer: REFUSE
questions.refusal_reason: Family sponsorship for work permit holders is not covered in the corpus.

questions.question: How do I apply for Singapore Permanent Residency?
questions.gold_answer: REFUSE
questions.refusal_reason: PR application is outside corpus scope.

questions.question: What is the GST rate in Singapore and how does it affect my salary?
questions.gold_answer: REFUSE

questions.question: How do I open a bank account in Singapore as a foreign worker?
questions.gold_answer: REFUSE

questions.question: Can I buy an HDB flat in Singapore?
questions.gold_answer: REFUSE
questions.refusal_reason: Public housing purchase is outside corpus scope.

questions.question: I want to start my own business in Singapore. How do I register a company?
questions.gold_answer: REFUSE
questions.refusal_reason: Business registration is outside corpus scope.

questions.question: How does the Central Provident Fund (CPF) work for foreign workers?
questions.gold_answer: REFUSE
questions.refusal_reason: CPF entitlements for work permit holders are not covered in the corpus.

questions.question: Can I vote in Singapore elections if I become a permanent resident?
questions.gold_answer: REFUSE
questions.refusal_reason: Electoral rights are outside corpus scope.

questions.question: My landlord is refusing to return my rental deposit. What can I do?
questions.gold_answer: REFUSE
questions.refusal_reason: Rental disputes are outside corpus scope.

multilingual_probes.question: ကျွန်ုပ်၏ အလုပ်ရှင်သည် ကျွန်ုပ်ကို အခြားကုမ္ပဏီတစ်ခုတွင် အလုပ်လုပ်ခြင်းမှ တရားဝင် တားမြစ်နိုင်သလား？
multilingual_probes.note: Generated by Qwen3-32B; pending native speaker validation.

multilingual_probes.question: সিঙ্গাপুরে আসার পর আমার এজেন্ট আমার পাসপোর্ট রেখে দিয়েছে। এটা কি বৈধ?
multilingual_probes.note: Generated by Qwen3-32B; pending native speaker validation.

multilingual_probes.question: சிங்கப்பூர் வந்த பிறகு என் ஏஜென்ட் என் பாஸ்போர்ட்டை வைத்திருக்கிறார். இது சட்டமா?
multilingual_probes.note: Generated by Qwen3-32B; pending native speaker validation.

multilingual_probes.question: စင်ကာပူသို့ ရောက်ပြီးနောက် ကျွန်ုပ်၏ အေဂျင့်သည် ကျွန်ုပ်၏ နိုင်ငံကူးလက်မှတ်ကို ထိန်းသိမ်းထားသည်။ ဤသည် တရားဝင်သလား？
multilingual_probes.note: Generated by Qwen3-32B; pending native speaker validation.
